speaker: EGEON
text: Proceed, Solinus, to procure my fall,

speaker: EGEON
text: And by the doom of death end woes and all.

speaker: DUKE
text: Merchant of Syracusa, plead no more.

speaker: DUKE
text: I am not partial to infringe our laws.

speaker: DUKE
text: The enmity and discord which of late

speaker: DUKE
text: Sprung from the rancorous outrage of your duke

speaker: DUKE
text: To merchants, our well-dealing countrymen,

speaker: DUKE
text: Who, wanting guilders to redeem their lives,

speaker: DUKE
text: Have sealed his rigorous statutes with their bloods,

speaker: DUKE
text: Excludes all pity from our threat’ning looks.

speaker: DUKE
text: For since the mortal and intestine jars

speaker: DUKE
text: ’Twixt thy seditious countrymen and us,

speaker: DUKE
text: It hath in solemn synods been decreed,

speaker: DUKE
text: Both by the Syracusians and ourselves,

speaker: DUKE
text: To admit no traffic to our adverse towns.

speaker: DUKE
text: Nay, more, if any born at Ephesus

speaker: DUKE
text: Be seen at Syracusian marts and fairs;

speaker: DUKE
text: Again, if any Syracusian born

speaker: DUKE
text: Come to the bay of Ephesus, he dies,

speaker: DUKE
text: His goods confiscate to the Duke’s dispose,

speaker: DUKE
text: Unless a thousand marks be levièd

speaker: DUKE
text: To quit the penalty and to ransom him.

speaker: DUKE
text: Thy substance, valued at the highest rate,

speaker: DUKE
text: Cannot amount unto a hundred marks;

speaker: DUKE
text: Therefore by law thou art condemned to die.

speaker: EGEON
text: Yet this my comfort: when your words are done,

speaker: EGEON
text: My woes end likewise with the evening sun.

speaker: DUKE
text: Well, Syracusian, say in brief the cause

speaker: DUKE
text: Why thou departedst from thy native home

speaker: DUKE
text: And for what cause thou cam’st to Ephesus.

speaker: EGEON
text: A heavier task could not have been imposed

speaker: EGEON
text: Than I to speak my griefs unspeakable;

speaker: EGEON
text: Yet, that the world may witness that my end

speaker: EGEON
text: Was wrought by nature, not by vile offense,

speaker: EGEON
text: I’ll utter what my sorrow gives me leave.

speaker: EGEON
text: In Syracusa was I born, and wed

speaker: EGEON
text: Unto a woman happy but for me,

speaker: EGEON
text: And by me, had not our hap been bad.

speaker: EGEON
text: With her I lived in joy. Our wealth increased

speaker: EGEON
text: By prosperous voyages I often made

speaker: EGEON
text: To Epidamium, till my factor’s death

speaker: EGEON
text: And the great care of goods at random left

speaker: EGEON
text: Drew me from kind embracements of my spouse;

speaker: EGEON
text: From whom my absence was not six months old

speaker: EGEON
text: Before herself—almost at fainting under

speaker: EGEON
text: The pleasing punishment that women bear—

speaker: EGEON
text: Had made provision for her following me

speaker: EGEON
text: And soon and safe arrivèd where I was.

speaker: EGEON
text: There had she not been long but she became

speaker: EGEON
text: A joyful mother of two goodly sons,

speaker: EGEON
text: And, which was strange, the one so like the other

speaker: EGEON
text: As could not be distinguished but by names.

speaker: EGEON
text: That very hour, and in the selfsame inn,

speaker: EGEON
text: A mean woman was deliverèd

speaker: EGEON
text: Of such a burden, male twins, both alike.

speaker: EGEON
text: Those, for their parents were exceeding poor,

speaker: EGEON
text: I bought and brought up to attend my sons.

speaker: EGEON
text: My wife, not meanly proud of two such boys,

speaker: EGEON
text: Made daily motions for our home return.

speaker: EGEON
text: Unwilling, I agreed. Alas, too soon

speaker: EGEON
text: We came aboard.

speaker: EGEON
text: A league from Epidamium had we sailed

speaker: EGEON
text: Before the always-wind-obeying deep

speaker: EGEON
text: Gave any tragic instance of our harm;

speaker: EGEON
text: But longer did we not retain much hope,

speaker: EGEON
text: For what obscurèd light the heavens did grant

speaker: EGEON
text: Did but convey unto our fearful minds

speaker: EGEON
text: A doubtful warrant of immediate death,

speaker: EGEON
text: Which though myself would gladly have embraced,

speaker: EGEON
text: Yet the incessant weepings of my wife,

speaker: EGEON
text: Weeping before for what she saw must come,

speaker: EGEON
text: And piteous plainings of the pretty babes,

speaker: EGEON
text: That mourned for fashion, ignorant what to fear,

speaker: EGEON
text: Forced me to seek delays for them and me.

speaker: EGEON
text: And this it was, for other means was none:

speaker: EGEON
text: The sailors sought for safety by our boat

speaker: EGEON
text: And left the ship, then sinking-ripe, to us.

speaker: EGEON
text: My wife, more careful for the latter-born,

speaker: EGEON
text: Had fastened him unto a small spare mast,

speaker: EGEON
text: Such as seafaring men provide for storms.

speaker: EGEON
text: To him one of the other twins was bound,

speaker: EGEON
text: Whilst I had been like heedful of the other.

speaker: EGEON
text: The children thus disposed, my wife and I,

speaker: EGEON
text: Fixing our eyes on whom our care was fixed,

speaker: EGEON
text: Fastened ourselves at either end the mast

speaker: EGEON
text: And, floating straight, obedient to the stream,

speaker: EGEON
text: Was carried towards Corinth, as we thought.

speaker: EGEON
text: At length the sun, gazing upon the earth,

speaker: EGEON
text: Dispersed those vapors that offended us,

speaker: EGEON
text: And by the benefit of his wished light

speaker: EGEON
text: The seas waxed calm, and we discoverèd

speaker: EGEON
text: Two ships from far, making amain to us,

speaker: EGEON
text: Of Corinth that, of Epidaurus this.

speaker: EGEON
text: But ere they came—O, let me say no more!

speaker: EGEON
text: Gather the sequel by that went before.

speaker: DUKE
text: Nay, forward, old man. Do not break off so,

speaker: DUKE
text: For we may pity though not pardon thee.

speaker: EGEON
text: O, had the gods done so, I had not now

speaker: EGEON
text: Worthily termed them merciless to us.

speaker: EGEON
text: For, ere the ships could meet by twice five leagues,

speaker: EGEON
text: We were encountered by a mighty rock,

speaker: EGEON
text: Which being violently borne upon,

speaker: EGEON
text: Our helpful ship was splitted in the midst;

speaker: EGEON
text: So that, in this unjust divorce of us,

speaker: EGEON
text: Fortune had left to both of us alike

speaker: EGEON
text: What to delight in, what to sorrow for.

speaker: EGEON
text: Her part, poor soul, seeming as burdenèd

speaker: EGEON
text: With lesser weight, but not with lesser woe,

speaker: EGEON
text: Was carried with more speed before the wind,

speaker: EGEON
text: And in our sight they three were taken up

speaker: EGEON
text: By fishermen of Corinth, as we thought.

speaker: EGEON
text: At length, another ship had seized on us

speaker: EGEON
text: And, knowing whom it was their hap to save,

speaker: EGEON
text: Gave healthful welcome to their shipwracked guests,

speaker: EGEON
text: And would have reft the fishers of their prey

speaker: EGEON
text: Had not their bark been very slow of sail;

speaker: EGEON
text: And therefore homeward did they bend their course.

speaker: EGEON
text: Thus have you heard me severed from my bliss,

speaker: EGEON
text: That by misfortunes was my life prolonged

speaker: EGEON
text: To tell sad stories of my own mishaps.

speaker: DUKE
text: And for the sake of them thou sorrowest for,

speaker: DUKE
text: Do me the favor to dilate at full

speaker: DUKE
text: What have befall’n of them and thee till now.

speaker: EGEON
text: My youngest boy, and yet my eldest care,

speaker: EGEON
text: At eighteen years became inquisitive

speaker: EGEON
text: After his brother, and importuned me

speaker: EGEON
text: That his attendant—so his case was like,

speaker: EGEON
text: Reft of his brother, but retained his name—

speaker: EGEON
text: Might bear him company in the quest of him,

speaker: EGEON
text: Whom whilst I labored of a love to see,

speaker: EGEON
text: I hazarded the loss of whom I loved.

speaker: EGEON
text: Five summers have I spent in farthest Greece,

speaker: EGEON
text: Roaming clean through the bounds of Asia,

speaker: EGEON
text: And, coasting homeward, came to Ephesus,

speaker: EGEON
text: Hopeless to find, yet loath to leave unsought

speaker: EGEON
text: Or that or any place that harbors men.

speaker: EGEON
text: But here must end the story of my life;

speaker: EGEON
text: And happy were I in my timely death

speaker: EGEON
text: Could all my travels warrant me they live.

speaker: DUKE
text: Hapless Egeon, whom the fates have marked

speaker: DUKE
text: To bear the extremity of dire mishap,

speaker: DUKE
text: Now, trust me, were it not against our laws,

speaker: DUKE
text: Against my crown, my oath, my dignity,

speaker: DUKE
text: Which princes, would they, may not disannul,

speaker: DUKE
text: My soul should sue as advocate for thee.

speaker: DUKE
text: But though thou art adjudgèd to the death,

speaker: DUKE
text: And passèd sentence may not be recalled

speaker: DUKE
text: But to our honor’s great disparagement,

speaker: DUKE
text: Yet will I favor thee in what I can.

speaker: DUKE
text: Therefore, merchant, I’ll limit thee this day

speaker: DUKE
text: To seek thy life by beneficial help.

speaker: DUKE
text: Try all the friends thou hast in Ephesus;

speaker: DUKE
text: Beg thou, or borrow, to make up the sum,

speaker: DUKE
text: And live. If no, then thou art doomed to die.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Jailer, take him to thy custody.

speaker: JAILER
text: I will, my lord.

speaker: EGEON
text: Hopeless and helpless doth Egeon wend,

speaker: EGEON
text: But to procrastinate his lifeless end.
